Reorder Nodes in HOOK
Reorder the nodes in the HOOK to ensure that the condition is evaluated before the assignment. This can be done by changing the order of the operands in the HOOK node. Specifically, when creating the HOOK node, the condition should be added as the first child, followed by the then branch, and then the else branch. This will ensure that the condition is evaluated before the assignment, avoiding any issues with side effects.

Use Temporary Variable
Use a temporary variable to hold the result of the condition, and then use this variable in the assignment. This would avoid any issues with side effects in the condition. The temporary variable can be created and assigned the result of the condition, and then this variable can be used in the HOOK node. This approach would ensure that the condition is evaluated only once and the result is used in the assignment, avoiding any issues with side effects.

Check for Side Effects and Avoid Transformation
Add a check to see if the condition has any side effects, and if so, avoid transforming the IF-ELSE statement into a HOOK. This would prevent any incorrect transformations and ensure that the code behaves correctly. The check can be done by analyzing the condition node and checking if it has any side effects, such as increment or decrement operations. If side effects are found, the transformation can be skipped, and the original IF-ELSE statement can be left unchanged.
